name: Beam Bridge
descr: Simply supported or continuous beam — precast or in-situ concrete/steel

name: T-Girder / Deck Girder
descr: Reinforced or prestressed concrete T-section girders — common on NSW/VIC rural roads (Austroads AGBT)

name: Box Girder
descr: Hollow rectangular cross-section — prestressed concrete or steel

name: Prestressed Concrete
descr: Super-T, I-girder, U-beam or in-situ prestressed systems (AS 5100.5)

name: Slab Bridge
descr: Solid or voided reinforced concrete deck spanning directly on abutments — short spans on local roads

name: Truss Bridge
descr: Triangulated steel truss — Warren / Pratt / Howe

name: Arch Bridge
column4: load carried in compression — scenic, urban and heritage crossings (AS 5100.3)

name: Masonry Arch
descr: Stone or brick masonry arch — heritage bridges

name: Portal Frame
descr: Rigid frame with legs integral with deck — culvert-style box or inclined leg portal (AS 5100.3)

name: Cable-Stayed
descr: Deck suspended by cables attached directly to towers — long-span highway bridges

name: Suspension Bridge
descr: Deck suspended from main catenary cables over towers — major long-span crossings

name: Cantilever Bridge
descr: Cantilevered sections from piers joined at midspan — Gerber or balanced cantilever (AS 5100.3)

name: Through Girder / Pony Truss
descr: Deck runs through the structural frame rather than on top — common on older rail bridges

name: Timber Bridge
descr: Hardwood or treated timber deck, stringers and girders — rural heritage bridges

name: Culvert Structure
descr: Buried RC box, arch or pipe culvert — headwalls and wingwalls only above ground (AS 1597)

name: Viaduct / Multi-Span
descr: Long multi-span elevated structure crossing a valley or urban precinct

name: Movable Bridge
descr: Bascule, swing, lift or pontoon — opening mechanism for marine traffic clearance
